[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Type: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `Config.ShutdownTimeout` being a `time.Duration` with no maximum cap to set an extremely large value, causing the Rosetta server to hang indefinitely on shutdown while holding signed but unsubmitted fund-transfer transactions in memory, which are then lost when the process is forcibly killed?
